items.title: John Ternus prepares to take over Apple after Tim Cook resignation
items.standfirst: Tim Cook announced he would be stepping down as Apple CEO on Monday. Jo Ling Kent reports on John Ternus, the mechanical engineer preparing to take the wheel.
items.content: Tim Cook announced he would be stepping down as Apple CEO on Monday. Jo Ling Kent reports on John Ternus, the mechanical engineer preparing to take the wheel.

items.title: Four key takeaways from Apple’s change of leadership
items.standfirst: Analysts say next boss John Ternus should diversify tech giant away from iPhones and raise its game in AIJohn Ternus takes over from Tim Cook as chief executive of Apple in September. A company insider, Ternus is moving up from his role as head of engineering to take control of the entire $4tn (£3tn) business.Apple is a vast, successful tech company and one of the most recognised brands in the world. But it faces challenges nonetheless. Here is a look at Ternus’s in-tray. Continue reading...

items.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple chief as John Ternus named replacement
items.standfirst: Cook, who will stay on as executive chair, praises head of hardware engineering, who will take over on 1 SeptemberApple announced on Monday that it had named a replacement for Tim Cook as CEO after nearly 15 years, with head of hardware engineering John Ternus succeeding him on 1 September. Cook will stay at the company in the role of executive chair.“It has been the greatest privilege of my life to be the CEO of Apple and to have been trusted to lead such an extraordinary company. I love Apple with all of my being,” Cook said in a press release. Continue reading...
items.content: Cook, who will stay on as executive chair, praises head of hardware engineering, who will take over on 1 SeptemberApple announced on Monday that it had named a replacement for Tim Cook as CEO after nearly 15 years, with head of hardware engineering John Ternus succeeding him on 1 September. Cook will stay at the company in the role of executive chair.“It has been the greatest privilege of my life to be the CEO of Apple and to have been trusted to lead such an extraordinary company. I love Apple with all of my being,” Cook said in a press release. Continue reading...

items.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook Is Stepping Down
items.standfirst: John Ternus, the company’s senior vice president of hardware engineering, will replace Cook as CEO on September 1. Cook will stay on as executive chairman.
items.content: John Ternus, the company’s senior vice president of hardware engineering, will replace Cook as CEO on September 1. Cook will stay on as executive chairman.

clusters.title: EU gives preliminary approval to unblock 90-bn-euro Ukraine loan
clusters.neutral_headline: EU Approves 90 Billion Euro Loan for Ukraine

items.title: EU gives preliminary approval to unblock 90-bn-euro Ukraine loan
items.standfirst: EU member states on Wednesday gave a preliminary approval to unlock a €90 billion loan for Ukraine. The decision comes after delays linked to an oil dispute over the Druzhba pipeline, with Hungary vetoing the funding.
items.content: EU member states on Wednesday gave a preliminary approval to unlock a €90 billion loan for Ukraine. The decision comes after delays linked to an oil dispute over the Druzhba pipeline, with Hungary vetoing the funding.

items.title: EU approves €90bn loan for Ukraine as pipeline is turned on ending deadlock
items.standfirst: Ukraine says it has reopened the Druzhba pipeline, after months of stalemate over stalled oil supplies to neighbouring Hungary.
items.content: Ukraine says it has reopened the Druzhba pipeline, after months of stalemate over stalled oil supplies to neighbouring Hungary.

ai_summary: EU member states approved a 90 billion euro loan
The loan was previously delayed due to an oil dispute
Hungary had vetoed the funding over pipeline issues
Ukraine has reopened the Druzhba pipeline
The loan will support Ukraine's economic development

clusters.title: Starmer says unaware of security lapse when appointing Mandelson
clusters.neutral_headline: Starmer Unaware of Security Lapse in Mandelson Appointment

items.title: Starmer says unaware of security lapse when appointing Mandelson
items.standfirst: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Wednesday told parliament he was unaware that Peter Mandelson was flagged by security checks prior to his appointment as envoy to the US. The remarks came after Olly Robbins, a senior foreign ministry official who was fired last week, said that Starmer had a "dismissive attitude" towards the security vetting process.
items.content: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Wednesday told parliament he was unaware that Peter Mandelson was flagged by security checks prior to his appointment as envoy to the US. The remarks came after Olly Robbins, a senior foreign ministry official who was fired last week, said that Starmer had a "dismissive attitude" towards the security vetting process.

clusters.title: El Salvador holds mass trial for 486 alleged gang members
clusters.neutral_headline: El Salvador Holds Mass Trial for Alleged Gang Members

items.title: El Salvador holds mass trial for 486 alleged gang members
items.standfirst: A Salvadoran court on Tuesday began a collective trial of 486 alleged gang members, one of the biggest mass trials under President Nayib Bukele’s crackdown on gang violence through controversial emergency powers
items.content: A Salvadoran court on Tuesday began a collective trial of 486 alleged gang members, one of the biggest mass trials under President Nayib Bukele’s crackdown on gang violence through controversial emergency powers

items.title: El Salvador holds mass trial for 486 alleged members of notorious MS-13 gang
items.standfirst: Human rights groups have warned that the collective prosecutions violate due process and block defendants from accessing legal counselA Salvadoran court on Tuesday began a collective trial of 486 alleged gang members, in one of the biggest mass trials under president Nayib Bukele’s crackdown on gang violence through controversial emergency powers.Prosecutors say the charges against alleged members of the Mara Salvatrucha gang, or MS-13, span more than 47,000 crimes committed between 2012 and 2022, including a weekend that was El Salvador’s bloodiest since its civil war. Continue reading...
items.content: Human rights groups have warned that the collective prosecutions violate due process and block defendants from accessing legal counselA Salvadoran court on Tuesday began a collective trial of 486 alleged gang members, in one of the biggest mass trials under president Nayib Bukele’s crackdown on gang violence through controversial emergency powers.Prosecutors say the charges against alleged members of the Mara Salvatrucha gang, or MS-13, span more than 47,000 crimes committed between 2012 and 2022, including a weekend that was El Salvador’s bloodiest since its civil war. Continue reading...

ai_summary: El Salvador is holding a mass trial for 486 alleged gang members
The trial is one of the largest under emergency powers
The alleged gang members face over 47,000 charges
The charges include crimes committed between 2012 and 2022
Human rights groups have warned about due process violations

clusters.title: Trump may send Afghan allies who were promised new lives in the U.S. to Congo instead

items.title: Trump may send Afghan allies who were promised new lives in the U.S. to Congo instead
items.standfirst: Hundreds of Afghan refugees who helped the United States fight the Taliban may be sent to the Democratic Republic of Congo after the Trump administration halted their U.S. resettlement, an advocacy group said
items.content: Hundreds of Afghan refugees who helped the United States fight the Taliban may be sent to the Democratic Republic of Congo after the Trump administration halted their U.S. resettlement, an advocacy group said

items.title: Trump officials consider sending 1,100 Afghans who aided US forces to Congo
items.standfirst: Discussions reportedly come after Trump’s decision to stop initiative that allowed group to apply to resettle in the USThe Trump administration is in discussions to potentially send up to 1,100 Afghans who helped US forces during the war in Afghanistan to the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), a non-profit confirmed on Tuesday.The resettlement talks, first reported by the New York Times, come after Donald Trump’s decision to stop an initiative that allowed Afghans who assisted US war efforts to apply to resettle in the US. Continue reading...
items.content: Discussions reportedly come after Trump’s decision to stop initiative that allowed group to apply to resettle in the USThe Trump administration is in discussions to potentially send up to 1,100 Afghans who helped US forces during the war in Afghanistan to the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), a non-profit confirmed on Tuesday.The resettlement talks, first reported by the New York Times, come after Donald Trump’s decision to stop an initiative that allowed Afghans who assisted US war efforts to apply to resettle in the US. Continue reading...

The Trump administration halted their resettlement
Up to 1,100 Afghans may be sent to Congo

clusters.title: World's biggest condom maker set to raise prices due to Iran war
clusters.neutral_headline: Condom Prices May Rise Due to Iran War

items.title: World's biggest condom maker set to raise prices due to Iran war
items.standfirst: Malaysia-based Karex produces more than five billion condoms a year and supplies global brands like Durex and Trojan.
items.content: Malaysia-based Karex produces more than five billion condoms a year and supplies global brands like Durex and Trojan.

items.title: Condom prices could rise 30% due to Iran war, says world’s top producer Karex
items.standfirst: Karex produces more than 5 billion condoms annually and is a supplier to leading brands like Durex and Trojan, as well as the NHS The world’s top condom producer, Malaysia’s Karex Bhd, plans to raise prices by 20% to 30% and possibly further if supply chain disruptions drag on due to the Iran war, its chief executive has said.Karex is also seeing a surge in condom demand as rising freight costs and shipping delays have left many of its customers with lower stockpiles than usual, CEO Goh Miah Kiat told Reuters in an interview on Tuesday. Continue reading...
items.content: Karex produces more than 5 billion condoms annually and is a supplier to leading brands like Durex and Trojan, as well as the NHS The world’s top condom producer, Malaysia’s Karex Bhd, plans to raise prices by 20% to 30% and possibly further if supply chain disruptions drag on due to the Iran war, its chief executive has said.Karex is also seeing a surge in condom demand as rising freight costs and shipping delays have left many of its customers with lower stockpiles than usual, CEO Goh Miah Kiat told Reuters in an interview on Tuesday. Continue reading...

ai_summary: Condom prices may rise by 30 percent
The rise is due to supply chain disruptions from the Iran war
Karex is the world's top condom producer
The company produces over 5 billion condoms annually
The price rise may affect global condom supplies
